obligations.reason: A dedicated debug APB bus (s_apb_debug_bus) might access security-critical system control registers in apb_soc_ctrl without requiring debug authentication.
obligations.subject: Master on s_apb_debug_bus (debug interface)
obligations.object: apb_soc_ctrl registers (e.g., boot addresses, cluster control, clock selects)
obligations.expected_guard: debug_authorized state or a lock bit preventing writes when debug is not authenticated
obligations.candidate_path: s_apb_debug_bus → APB node (or direct) → apb_soc_ctrl PADDR/PWDATA/PSEL/PENABLE
state_conditions_to_check: debug authorization bit (if any) after reset
write enable generation in apb_soc_ctrl when PSEL is driven by debug bus vs. peripheral bus
obligations.uncertainty: Exact routing of s_apb_debug_bus to apb_soc_ctrl not visible; needs RTL inspection and connection tracing.

obligations.reason: apb_soc_ctrl has JTAG register interface (soc_jtag_reg_i/o) that could allow writing sensitive control registers without proper JTAG authentication.
obligations.subject: JTAG TAP via soc_jtag_reg_i
obligations.object: Internal registers driving fc_bootaddr_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, sel_fll_clk, cluster_rstn, etc.
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG authentication state or a lock bit that gates JTAG-driven writes to critical registers
obligations.candidate_path: soc_jtag_reg_i → internal JTAG-to-register bridge logic in apb_soc_ctrl → target register updates
state_conditions_to_check: JTAG lock/unlock condition (e.g., a register bit or external signal)
reset default of JTAG access enable
obligations.uncertainty: Need to see how soc_jtag_reg_i is driven and if any auth gating exists inside apb_soc_ctrl.

obligations.reason: The FLL configuration registers in apb_fll_if may have lock bits to protect clock settings, but debug/test paths or missing write protection could allow unauthorized modification.
obligations.subject: Any APB master (CPU, debug, DMA)
obligations.object: FLL control registers (frequency, lock, configuration) in apb_fll_if
obligations.expected_guard: A lock bit that, once set, prevents further writes until a reset or a special unlock sequence
obligations.candidate_path: APB write transaction → apb_fll_if → register update logic that may/may not check a lock bit
state_conditions_to_check: lock bit reset value (should be unlocked or locked?)
lock bit writeability after being set
bypass paths (e.g., debug bus writes ignoring lock)
obligations.uncertainty: Presence and exact behavior of FLL lock bit(s) need to be confirmed in RTL.

obligations.reason: Boot address registers (fc_bootaddr_o, cluster_boot_addr_o) determine code execution locations; they may be writable by any APB master without a security lock.
obligations.subject: Any APB master (e.g., CPU core, DMA, debug)
obligations.object: fc_bootaddr_o and cluster_boot_addr_o registers in apb_soc_ctrl
obligations.expected_guard: A lock bit or privilege check preventing modification after secure boot, or restricted to a trusted master
obligations.candidate_path: APB write to apb_soc_ctrl address range → register assignment to bootaddr outputs
state_conditions_to_check: reset default of boot addresses (safe vs. attacker-controlled)
existence of a lock bit or write-protect mechanism
write gating conditions (e.g., lifecycle state, debug mode)
obligations.uncertainty: Not known if any lock or lifecycle gating exists; needs code inspection.

obligations.reason: Debug XBAR buses (s_lint_debug_bus, s_lint_jtag_bus) may allow read/write access to TCDM or other internal memories, potentially exposing sensitive data or enabling code injection.
obligations.subject: Debug interface (lint_debug, lint_jtag) via XBAR_TCDM_BUS
obligations.object: TCDM memory or other internal memory regions
obligations.expected_guard: Debug authentication state that blocks memory accesses when not authorized
obligations.candidate_path: s_lint_debug_bus/s_lint_jtag_bus → soc_interconnect → memory controller → on-chip SRAM
state_conditions_to_check: debug authentication enable signal
default state of debug memory access after reset
any address range restrictions on debug bus
obligations.uncertainty: Bus connections and any authentication logic inside interconnect are not visible; need to examine RTL.

obligations.reason: The udma_subsystem (DMA engine) may have unrestricted access to the memory map, potentially reading/writing protected memory regions (e.g., secure boot code, cryptographic keys).
obligations.subject: udma_subsystem (DMA)
obligations.object: Any memory region, especially protected assets
obligations.expected_guard: PMP-like range checks or access control configuration that limits DMA accesses to allowed memory ranges
obligations.candidate_path: udma_subsystem master ports → soc_interconnect → memory slaves
state_conditions_to_check: DMA source/destination address generation logic
any range-checking or security configuration registers
default permission after reset (open vs. closed)
obligations.uncertainty: No visibility into internal address-check logic; it may or may not exist. Need to verify in RTL.

obligations.reason: GPIO pad direction and output registers (apb_gpio) could control security-critical external signals; their writeability by untrusted masters must be protected.
obligations.subject: Any APB master
obligations.expected_guard: A lock bit or privilege check that prevents unauthorized GPIO configuration
obligations.candidate_path: APB write transaction → apb_gpio → register file update without a lock check
state_conditions_to_check: lock bit existence and reset value
write access to lock bit itself
bypass via debug bus
obligations.uncertainty: GPIO pins may or may not be security-critical; assume potential impact, need to examine actual connections.

obligations.reason: The APB node (apb_node) may merge transactions from multiple masters (e.g., peripheral bus and debug bus) without distinguishing security attributes, allowing a debug master to access peripherals intended only for the main CPU.
obligations.subject: APB node with multiple masters (NB_MASTER > 1)
obligations.object: Downstream APB slaves (apb_fll_if, apb_gpio, apb_soc_ctrl, etc.)
obligations.expected_guard: Separate address decode or security signal that blocks debug master from accessing certain slaves/regions
obligations.candidate_path: Master inputs → APB node internal mux/decode → psel_o to slaves without master-ID filtering
state_conditions_to_check: Address mapping for each master
Any master-ID-dependent decode logic
Default psel generation when multiple masters assert
obligations.uncertainty: Master-side separation and any access control beyond address decode are unknown; need RTL analysis.
